sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम।
देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
sudur-darśham idaṁ rūpaṁ dṛṣṭavān asi yan mama
devā apy asya rūpasya nityaṁ darśana-kāṅkṣiṇaḥ
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: This form of Mine which you have seen is very hard to behold; even the gods ever long to see it.
contextual_meaning: After the terrifying yet awe-inspiring vision of the universal form, Krishna reassures Arjuna that what he has just witnessed is extraordinarily rare. The statement functions as both validation and gentle closure: Arjuna’s eyes have been granted entry into a reality that even celestial beings crave but seldom attain. Psychologically, it is the moment when the overwhelming archetypal encounter is framed as a privileged revelation, helping the ego re-integrate after being dwarfed by the Self.

shadow: Fear of abandonment by the divine

psychological_state: Post-awe integration: the ego, having been temporarily dissolved in the presence of the numinous, is now being re-stabilized through affirmation of grace.
trigger_scenario: A patient who has just broken through into a transpersonal peak experience during therapy—seeing vivid archetypal imagery, feeling both terror and bliss—now asks, "Was that real? Why me?" The therapist normalizes the rarity of the experience while honoring its authenticity.
anchor_text: Cherish the rare vision that even gods desire.
